author: @leostarling5746
text: Archimedes has high torque, whereas Pelton has high speed. The generator design has to match one or the other. With gearless generator, you must go for speed. Otherwise your generator will be always under utilized. So you should be concentrating on the question: what gives me the maximum rotational speed in a small package? Always testing in very light winds is not addressing this. It is only answering: 'what has enough torque to get going at all?'

Dear Robert,
Thank you
I’ve been looking at what’s about today,
this turbine is like one, we have which till recently was used for forcing air, in a continuous grain dryer very much like that 
without cone,
motor attached 
Gigantic in comparison 
Spins well
Outer casing still on though , leading too down through type system 
I could remove casing add cone, face too west
I’ll load video 12 days or so.
States on motor: Volts 389/420
Phase 3 
Rev per min 1470
36 Amps
So you see if it could spin , in idle on a low breeze
Like vent fans do in an unused building 
and sometimes hard in a going one
What the dickens will it do left for a year if I pay too plug it into supply 
That is the thing we are all after
Got me thinking 
Interested one,
Henry 
❤

author: @earthlingthings
text: Only just found 4:43  a 3d print service here. (India). Cant wait to get the final version printed. I kinda hope youd have the darwin catcher above. The version of blades in this video, the upgraded screw cone in the centre. The taller serpentine coil arrangement you made in a previous video, where u stretched out the vertical sections. Finally, The maglev bearing at the base. All scaled to slot together. Ps: if you put the magnets on the blades here, and put the coil around it, you might even kill two birds with one stone. Of course, thered be gaps for the wind to pass through and out past the coil. And since the darwin is feeding from centre out, youd lose nothing by doing so

author: @corringhamdepot4434
text: I was amused to learn about the Soviet Union "Kerosene Lamp Generator". When they first wanted to spread propaganda by radio, many remote areas had no access to electricity. So they made a heat difference generator that sat on top of their oil lamp. Which would put out enough power for a radio. I wondered if you could now make an emergency phone charger, that would work sitting over a candle flame? 🤔

author: @Smo1k
text: I see a few comments suggesting to exchange the central cone with an Archimedes screw... I don't think it's a good idea: Yes, it'll help the Pelton design to get started, but then it'll run into the problem that the Archimedes screw is most efficient in a heavy medium with large force per speed, like water, while the Pelton design is a fast runner, better in low viscosity and mass, and high speed. When the Pelton nears its best, the Archimedes design will be holding it back as it goes from being moved by, to moving, the air.

author: @MichalKottman
text: This looks nice! Do you eventually plan to compare your designs under the same load, how they compare? I have a feeling that "generates high open voltage" might not be the best benchmark of power generation. My intuition is that if something is easy to spin up, then actual load might also spin it down easily. High voltage can easily be achieved by putting lots of tiny solar cells in series, but that doesn't automatically mean they will output a lot of power.

text:
Would it be possible to try the Archimedes Spiral ..... backwards?
Apparently you get more lift from a wing due to the air pressure being lower on its upper surface than you get from the air being forced downwards by the wing passing!
I have not got the figures to hand, but it occurs to me the Spiral may act in the same way,so ........ worth a try?

text: I don't know about this blade type.  It's a new concept and that's for sure.  I wonder what Bernoulli's, Euler and Convective acceleration equation results would be for this.  Fluid mechanics is just a bit out of my normal zone.  I have crossed into this on ion thrusters designs.  One I knew would work failed on engine mass to thrust ratio.  Yes, it upped it but failed because it was too bloody heavy.  I tabled it to rethink about it later.  That was 30 years ago.

author: @bartronicsecurity
text: What happens if you put an Archemedes screw in the middle of a Pelton wheel instead of a cone? As I see it the Archemedes screw will take the wind at first and then it gets forced further by the Pelton wheel. How much more energy do you get then? Obviously you have to check the direction of the blades of the Pelton wheel in order for them not to work against each other.

author: @atheistboomer7700
text: It would have been interesting to have seen a direct comparison of these 2 devices, by having them hooked up to your meter to see which harvested the most energy from a known head of air

replies.author: @danedmiston9673
replies.text: There have been several request for direct comparisons on this channel.  It seems like the most logical way to compare turbines of the same size.    One problem is that the test equipment must be very consistent.  The wind direct from the blower is spinning and very turbulent.   The same turbine can show very different results from day to day.

replies.author: @atheistboomer7700
replies.text:
@danedmiston9673 True, but I have seen Robert use a wind speed meter, in previous videos. That could be used as well, if the wind source is that unreliable.
I’m not looking for precise, publishable data here, but knowing that one device gives x2 the output of the other, under the same conditions, would help to focus the research.

author: @burnthetv007
text: There’s not much in this but my favourite mind blowing free energy tech toy is a glass lightbulb with a small bladed metal fan inside. The blades are angled in such a way and are coloured black on one side. The Sun hits these and powers it round as they are absorbed and cling to it  and they push the white side away in the same direction. I just love seeing the particles of sunlight effecting a surface like that! I forget name of it. I would love to see how much use they COULD be larger scale and WöW to stand in one!!

author: @fpvangel4495
text:
On solar at night ... on a 6v panel i was running an air pump which reverses polarity at night and becomes a water pump, there is a day and night polarity switch i discovered, add a full wave bridge rectifier and voila - night solar lamp, i havent tried to run a light but would like to see you experiment with that. 

1.9v was the max i noticed at night on a small 6v panel, the motor only needed 1.5v to start it.

FPV.
